diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Septic joint, atypical infection, Lyme disease
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient had a long history of right knee osteoarthritis and presented with worsening pain following a Synvisc injection, along with subjective fevers and chills. Diagnostic workup including elevated inflammatory markers, joint aspiration showing elevated WBC counts, and consultation by Infectious Disease suggested a possible septic joint. However, cultures were negative and the reasoning indicated that the presentation may relate to an inflammatory process rather than infection alone.
